title: Simple low-cost Synthesis of crosslinker DHEBA (N,N′-(1,2-dihydroxyethylene)bis-acrylamide)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"c1dt4","text":"For expansion microscopy and iterative ultrastructure expansion microscopy (ExM, iU-ExM) (16-22 times magnification), a crosslinker that is dissolvable in pH \u003e 13.3 is useful. DHEBA crosslinker (N,N′-(1,2-dihydroxyethylene)bis-acrylamide) is a commonly used crosslinker, usually in the range of 0.05% to 0.1% w/v% concentration. There are cost, purity and lead-time issues with DHEBA purchased from vendors. (All-optical visualization of specific molecules in the ultrastructural context of brain tissue | Nature Biotechnology).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":409,"length":117}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"4999A81D307A11F186F70A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://www.nature.com/articles/s41587-025-02905-4#Sec10"}}}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"7qbae","text":"The protocol is intended for non-commercial research use only.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"2l1j0","text":"Both Acrylamide and NaOH are either carcinogenic, corrosive, toxic, and harmful. Glyoxal is moderately toxic, glyoxal is flammable. DHEBA is predicted to be similarly at least as hazardous as acrylamide and glyoxal. Handle under a fume hood with appropriate PPE.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"c93r4","text":"Acrylamide 40% w/v Electrophoresis grade (Bio-Rad 1610140)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"almti","text":"Sodium Hydroxide 10N","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1jmf7","text":"Glyoxal 40 wt.% water (Sigma-Aldrich) ← note we successfully used an unopened decades-old bottle, or TCI America 107-22-2","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7otka","text":"pH test strips","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Beginning with Acrylamide 40 Mass / % volume , initially around pH 5 . In a fume hood, bring pH between pH 8 to pH 9 using NaOH 10 Molarity (M) . Do not use an electrode pH meter on acrylamide.

Glyoxal 40 Mass Percent in water is viscous and a density of 1.24 g/mL. It is most accurate to mass out 2.9 mg/mL of desired volume. Ex. to make 10 mL of mixed solution (39% acrylamide 0.4% crosslinker w/v%), mass out 29 mg of glyoxal 40% per 10mL total solution.
Mix Glyoxal and Acrylamide in closed Falcon tube and keep shaking at Room temperature for 02:00:00 . Protect from light.
Store at 4 °C protected from light. The solution lasts at least a month in these conditions if not longer.
creator: Rocky An
